title: CSRF token comparison in Middleware#internal_call is not constant-time
body: internal_call compares request.cookies[CSRF_TOKEN_COOKIE_NAME] to body['csrfToken'] with plain ==, which is vulnerable to a timing attack against the error-page CSRF token. Please use Rack::Utils.secure_compare or ActiveSupport::SecurityUtils.secure_compare instead.

title: IPv6 loopback allow-listing is silently skipped on Ruby builds without IPv6
body: Middleware's ALLOWED_IPS setup does `allow_ip! "::1/128" rescue nil` to work around Ruby builds without IPv6 support, but nothing logs when that fallback triggers, so requests from ::1 can be quietly rejected with no clue why.

title: Add VS Code Insiders / Cursor to the known editor presets
body: Editor::KNOWN_EDITORS only sniffs vscode/vscodium via the /code/i and /codium/i patterns, so setting BETTER_ERRORS_EDITOR=cursor or code-insiders falls through to the TextMate default instead of opening the right app.

title: editor_spec does not cover BETTER_ERRORS_EDITOR_URL overriding EDITOR
body: Editor.default_editor checks BETTER_ERRORS_EDITOR_URL before BETTER_ERRORS_EDITOR/EDITOR, but editor_spec.rb has no example asserting the URL variable wins when both are set at once.

title: no_errors_json_response framework hints are outdated for newer Shotgun/Hanami releases
body: no_errors_json_response in the middleware hardcodes explanations for Middleman, Shotgun and Hanami reloading, but the message doesn't mention Puma's phased restart or Rails' newer code reloader, which are now the more common cause of a stale error session.
